Definition: accessory olfactory bulb (total = measured layers 1+2 + 3-5 + 6)
Note: Latin synonym Bulbus_olfactorius_accessorius; distinct from the MAIN olfactory bulb (MOB)

Definition: species name as printed in Stephan et al. 1982 (classification of the day; superscript removed)
Note: single "species" column in the snapshot also carries the grade/family/Mean labels as rows; taxonomy applied later via ../_keys/Stephan/species_key.csv

Definition: the name superscript printed in the journal (1-11), if any
Note: kept as a superscript in the snapshot; turned into this reference number by the R script

Definition: former name used in earlier papers and in Stephan, Frahm & Baron (1981), per the journal legend
Note: 1 Aethechinus algirus; 2 Crocidura occidentalis; 3 Nesogale dobsoni; 4 Nesogale talazaci; 5 Chlorotalpa stuhlmanni; 6 Rhynchocyon stuhlmanni; 7 Lemur fulvus; 8 Lemur variegatus; 9 Galago crassicaudatus; 10 Galago demidovii; 11 Saguinus tamarin

Definition: number of individuals (sample size)
Note: snapshot column 'n' (col 1); marker carried in n_note

Definition: sample-size marker printed next to n
Note: * / bullet next to n in the scan; see paper legend

Definition: absolute total AOB volume in mm3 (both sides)
standard error of the mean of the AOB volume as a percentage of the mean
Note: snapshot 'SEM_pct' (col 3); printed only for n>1 (blank for n=1); empirical (transcribed from the scan)

Definition: relative (allometric) size of the total AOB; 100 = the basal-Insectivora reference line
AOB volume as per mille (0/00) of net brain volume
Note: snapshot 'permille_net_brain' (col 5); header symbol may read %0 or 0/00 (= per mille)

Definition: AOB volume as per mille (0/00) of main-olfactory-bulb (MOB) volume
volume of AOB layers 1+2 (combined) in mm3
volume of AOB layers 3-5 (combined) in mm3
volume of AOB layer 6 in mm3
AOB layers 1+2 as a percentage of total AOB
AOB layers 3-5 as a percentage of total AOB
AOB layer 6 as a percentage of total AOB
relative (allometric) size of AOB layers 1+2; 100 = basal-Insectivora reference line
relative (allometric) size of AOB layers 3-5; 100 = basal-Insectivora reference line
relative (allometric) size of AOB layer 6; 100 = basal-Insectivora reference line

size index = 100 x observed / expected; expected from a reference line of fixed slope 0.57 (log-log) drawn through the mean log-volume and mean log-body-weight of the 'basal' Insectivora (n=12). All basal points average to 100 on the line.
Note: Stephan, Baron & Frahm 1982 Methods (p.5); body weights from Stephan et al. 1981. Reconstructed indices reproduce every legible printed value to +/-1 and the printed basal mean (117) and grand mean (185) exactly.

Definition: number of species
Note: applies to the Mean rows of Table 1 (not included in this per-species table)
